If some paths include cross-module calls but other paths to endNode do NOT, generate advisory: YELLOW 'MF20-BYPASS-PATH: Module {A} calls {B} in path [{P1_nodes}] but not in path [{P2_nodes}]. Verify if path {P2} should also notify module {B}. Common cause: block/unblock or error paths that skip integration steps.' This is advisory-level (YELLOW) because not all paths require the same cross-module calls — some paths legitimately bypass certain modules. Include in MF20 report: bypass_paths: [{ module, signal_target, paths_with_call: [path_ids], paths_without_call: [path_ids] }]), MF21 (intra-function step mutual exclusion audit — verify 3+ steps modifying same output have shared gate variable; consumes MF18 conflict_matrix; no gate → YELLOW), MF22 (safety gate signal enforcement audit — verify safety module gate/throttle signals are checked by downstream modules; unenforced safety signals → RED non-waivable), MF23 (name-version embedded consistency), MF24 (protocol document consistency — scan evolution changes for new entities without protocol coverage), MF25 (tool integration completeness — verify all tool touchpoints updated when tools[] changed), MF26 (enum registry consistency — cross-module enum value coverage audit), MF27 (new module dependency symmetry — compare new module dependencies against existing patterns). Each MF violation counts as 1 issue in Intrinsic dimension of the main file's grade. SCORE TRAJECTORY ANALYSIS (B5 v1.21.0): For Evolve mode (when .evolution_snapshot/quality_baseline.json exists): (1) Load snapshot baseline scores (C, I, D, weighted). (2) After computing current scores, calculate per-dimension delta: delta_C = current_C - snapshot_C, delta_I = current_I - snapshot_I, delta_D = current_D - snapshot_D, delta_weighted = current_weighted - snapshot_weighted. (3) For each dimension with negative delta (regression), identify the specific check items that scored lower than snapshot and annotate with root cause: 'Score Impact: {dimension} {delta:+0.000} — caused by {check_id}: {description}'. (4) Include in test_report: score_trajectory = { snapshot_version, snapshot_scores: {C, I, D, weighted}, current_scores: {C, I, D, weighted}, deltas: {C, I, D, weighted}, regression_causes: [{ dimension, check_id, old_score, new_score, cause }], improvement_causes: [{ dimension, check_id, old_score, new_score, cause }] }. (5) Pass score_trajectory to ReviewStep for display in PresentDraft. SKIP for Create mode (no snapshot baseline).
ThreeDimTest.constraints: TIER-ADJUSTED SEVERITY: Simple -> I2 is WARNING (at least type guard required), I3 skip if no file_system. Standard/Professional -> I2 is FAIL (all 5 guards), I3 is FAIL with file_system. MULTI-FILE: MF violations are Intrinsic-severity. Report both per-file grades and composite score. COMPLEXITY ADVISORY: step3 (I1-I13 intrinsic checks) and step4 (D1-D10 detail checks) each contain 10+ evaluation items in a single step. This density mirrors the STANDARD checklist structure — splitting would fragment the evaluation context needed for cross-check correlation (e.g., I2 findings inform I8 scoring). DECOMPOSITION PLAN: If either dimension exceeds 15 check items, split into sub-steps: step3a (I1-I7 core intrinsic), step3b (I8-I13 security+ecosystem intrinsic). Current state: 13+10 items, at threshold but justified by evaluation context dependency.
ThreeDimTest.Error: Unparseable -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: partial grading -> inform.

TrafficClassify.step1: GREEN — auto-apply silently: Error field addition, input validation addition, boundary rule text, minor formatting.
TrafficClassify.step2: YELLOW — auto-apply + report: restructuring suggestions, metabolic cleaning, missing constraint additions.
TrafficClassify.step3: RED — pause for human: fractal restructuring, parameter space changes, flow redesign, grade drop >=2 levels. CROSS-FILE GRANULARITY: version/id-only changes across files -> GREEN. Same-module internal changes (e.g. generate + its helper) -> YELLOW. Cross-module data contract changes (e.g. research output format affects generate input) -> RED. AIAP-TOOLS CLASSIFICATION: trust_level violation in aiap_tools/ -> RED. governance_hash mismatch in aiap_tools/ -> RED. circular dependency detected -> RED. permissions excess in aiap_tools/ -> RED. sub-program missing optional fields -> YELLOW. recursion depth approaching limit (depth=2) -> YELLOW.
TrafficClassify.step4: Generate traffic_report: { green:[...], yellow:[...], red:[...], dim_grades:{C,I,D} }.
TrafficClassify.constraints: Cross-file classification per CROSS-FILE GRANULARITY rules in step3. Score impact >=1.0 -> RED.
TrafficClassify.Error: Classification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: default all items YELLOW -> inform.

PostGenCheck.step1: Check traffic_report for unresolved RED items. If present -> PauseHuman.
PostGenCheck.step2: Display summary: 'Green N | Yellow N | Red N | C:{g} I:{g} D:{g} | weighted:{score}'.
PostGenCheck.constraints: RED items only gate. Weighted score threshold is checked by main.aisop.json QualityGate after generation completes.
PostGenCheck.Error: Gate check fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat as RED present, route to PauseHuman -> inform.

PauseHuman.step1: Present each RED item: what/why/proposed-change/before-after. SUB_MERMAID DECOMPOSITION DISPLAY: when RED item has severity='RED_WITH_FIX' and contains sub_mermaid_proposal, present enhanced view: (a) PATTERN — show detected decomposition pattern (FAN_OUT/SEQUENTIAL/CONDITIONAL) with explanation, (b) BEFORE — show current Mermaid graph with node count and highlight nodes to be extracted, (c) AFTER MAIN — show proposed main Mermaid with SubRef delegation nodes replacing extracted clusters, (d) AFTER SUB — show each proposed sub-graph Mermaid with Entry routing and handler nodes, (e) NODE COUNT COMPARISON — table showing before (N nodes) vs after (main: M nodes + sub1: K1 nodes + sub2: K2 nodes), (f) FUNCTION MIGRATION — list which functions move from main to sub-graph, (g) RISK ASSESSMENT — note any cross-references that may break.
PauseHuman.step2: Wait: approve, reject (continue with warning), or modify. For sub_mermaid_proposal: [Apply] executes decomposition via ApplyFixes.step1_5, [Reject] keeps current structure with fractal_exempt advisory, [Modify] allows user to adjust split points before applying.
PauseHuman.constraints: Never auto-approve RED items. Sub_mermaid decomposition proposals MUST show full before/after diff preview before user decision.
PauseHuman.Error: Timeout -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat as rejected, SaveDraft with warnings -> inform.

ApplyFixes.step1: Apply GREEN silently. Apply YELLOW and log each.
ApplyFixes.step1_5: SUB_MERMAID DECOMPOSITION APPLY (B8 fix execution): When traffic_report contains RED_WITH_FIX item with fix_type='sub_mermaid_decomposition' AND human approved via PauseHuman: (1) BACKUP current aisop object (deep copy in-memory). (2) REPLACE main Mermaid — set aisop.mermaid to sub_mermaid_proposal.main_mermaid. (3) ADD SUB MERMAID — for each entry in sub_mermaid_proposal.sub_mermaids, create aisop.<name> object with { mermaid: entry.mermaid, functions: { Entry: entry.entry_function, ...entry.handler_functions } }. (4) ADD DELEGATE FUNCTIONS — for each delegate in sub_mermaid_proposal.delegate_functions, add to aisop.functions (the main graph's functions). (5) SET FRACTAL_EXEMPT — if sub_mermaid_proposal.fractal_exempt is non-null, add fractal_exempt field to the sub-graph's content object. (6) MIGRATE FUNCTIONS — move extracted handler functions from main functions{} to sub-graph functions{}. Remove from main, add to sub. Preserve all step/constraint/Error content exactly. (7) VERIFY — recount nodes in main and each sub-graph. Assert all <= 15. If verification fails, ROLLBACK to backup and report as RED unresolved. (8) LOG in generation_report: { sub_mermaid_applied: true, pattern: <pattern>, main_nodes_before: N, main_nodes_after: M, sub_graphs_created: [{ name, node_count }] }. POST-SPLIT NODE VERIFICATION STRENGTHENED (CR6): After any sub_mermaid decomposition execution, MANDATORY verification: (1) Count functional nodes in main_graph (Mermaid nodes minus Start, endNode). Verify count <= 16. (2) Count functional nodes in EACH sub_graph. Verify each count <= 16. (3) If ANY graph exceeds 16 nodes post-split: RED 'Post-split violation: {graph_name} has {N} nodes (> C5 threshold 16). Decomposition must be revised.' Abort decomposition and restore backup. (4) Record fractal_exempt documentation in module aisop metadata: 'fractal_exempt': '{total} nodes across {sub_count} sub_mermaid graphs ({graph_name1} {count1}, {graph_name2} {count2}), all under C5 threshold'. (5) RECURSIVE CHECK: if any sub_graph itself has a sub-delegate node, recursively verify that nested sub-graphs also satisfy C5 threshold.
ApplyFixes.step2: FIX ORDER: isolated -> local per-function -> cross-function one-at-a-time. MULTI-FILE ORDER: sub-modules first, main last.
ApplyFixes.step3: VERSION RULES per STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management: modify_only→patch+1, evolve_level_c_only→patch+1, evolve_level_b→minor+1, evolve_level_a→major+1, mixed→highest applicable level wins. NAME FIELD VERSION SYNC (CR9): When updating the 'version' field in .aisop.json files during evolution: (1) For each file, read the 'name' field. (2) If name contains a version pattern (matching regex /v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+/), replace the embedded version with the new version. Example: name='soulbot_chat v1.8.0' with new version '1.10.0' → name='soulbot_chat v1.10.0'. (3) If name does not contain a version pattern, leave name unchanged. (4) Record name_version_synced: true in pipeline_metadata for each synced file. This prevents MF23 violations from recurring after every evolution.
ApplyFixes.step4: DESCRIPTION HYGIENE: if description contains more than 2 version history entries (e.g. 'v1.1.0 adds... v1.2.0 adds... v1.3.0 adds...'), compress into summary: keep current version changes in full, collapse older entries into single line 'Prior versions added: [comma-separated feature list].' SUMMARY CONSISTENCY: after description update, verify summary field still reflects current capabilities. If evolution added key features (new metrics, new modules, new tools) that summary doesn't mention, update summary. If evolution removed features that summary still mentions, update summary. Summary should remain 1-2 sentences — do not add version history. DERIVED REFERENCE AUDIT: full-file scan for internal consistency — (a) NAME VERSION SYNC: if name field contains embedded vX.Y.Z, update to match version field. Apply to ALL files in multi-file packages. (b) ERROR TEXT CONSISTENCY: scan all Error/fallback fields for hardcoded counts. Cross-reference each against actual list lengths in same function's steps. Fix stale counts. (c) MENU COUNT SYNC: Start/General menu item counts → match actual NLU intent/feature count. (d) EXPLAIN FEATURE SYNC: Explain.step1 feature list count → match Explain.Error referenced count. (e) CROSS-FUNCTION REFERENCE CHECK: if function A references function B by name in steps/constraints, verify B exists in the same file's functions. Auto-fix any mismatches found. Log all fixes in generation_report: { derived_fixes: [{location, old_value, new_value}] }. SMART DESCRIPTION UPDATE (B3 v1.18.0): After DESCRIPTION HYGIENE and DERIVED REFERENCE AUDIT, perform automated capability-aware description update: (1) EXTRACT CURRENT CAPABILITIES: parse existing description field to identify capability phrases — scan for comma-separated feature lists, parenthetical version tags '(B{N} v{X.Y.Z})', and '+' separated additions. Build capabilities_current = [{ phrase, version_tag_if_any }]. (2) COMPUTE CAPABILITY DELTA: for each accepted LEVEL_B change, create capability_addition = { feature_name: change.title condensed to noun phrase, version_tag: '(B{N} v{new_version})' }. For changes that REPLACE existing functionality (same function + same step), mark old capability as superseded. (3) GENERATE UPDATED DESCRIPTION: retain non-superseded capability descriptions, replace superseded entries with new versions, append new additions, update version references. Verify summary remains <= 500 characters (D8-SUMMARY). (4) GENERATE UPDATED MODULE DESCRIPTIONS (AIAP.md): for each modified module, extract new LEVEL_B features targeting that module, update AIAP.md module description line by replacing old version-tagged features with new ones while preserving base role description. (5) SAFETY GATE: if auto-updated description differs from original by >50% character change ratio, classify as YELLOW for human review instead of auto-apply. Log: 'SMART DESCRIPTION UPDATE: {N} capabilities extracted, {M} additions, {K} superseded, description delta {pct}%'.
ApplyFixes.step5: CUSTOM FIELD SEMANTIC CHECK: scan for fields like verified_on, author, tags. Preserve all. For verified_on specifically: if content changed beyond cosmetic fixes, append '(not re-verified after vX.Y.Z evolution)' to description as advisory. Do NOT modify the verified_on array itself.
ApplyFixes.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: in-memory backup. ATOMIC: .tmp rename. Max 3 fix cycles.
ApplyFixes.Error: Apply fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display patched JSON inline -> inform.

SaveDraft.step1: DIRECTORY RULES: Modify -> save in-place at {workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/. Evolve -> save in-place (overwrite with .bak backup). Create -> create new directory {workspace_dir}/{name}_aiap/.
SaveDraft.step2: Save {file}.aisop.json. Atomic write: .tmp then rename. Backup existing as .v{old_version}.bak (e.g. main.aisop.json.v1.0.0.bak). AIAP.md CONTENT PREPARATION: if AIAP.md content was generated (Create mode) or updated (Evolve mode), prepare AIAP.md content but do NOT write — pass prepared content to ReviewStep for single-ownership write (per main.aisop.json ReviewStep.step3). MULTI-FILE: save each changed .aisop.json file with individual .bak. Unchanged files are NOT rewritten. INCREMENTAL: output content_hash for each written file in draft metadata for downstream stage-skip decisions. WRITE LOGGING: log write results in pipeline context: { files_written: [string], backups_created: [string], atomic_success: boolean, bytes_written: number }. If in pipeline context, append write_log to pipeline_metadata for ObservabilityStep telemetry. MODULE DESCRIPTION TEMPLATE (C3 v1.18.0): When preparing AIAP.md content (Create or Evolve mode), ensure each module description row follows the standard template format: '| **{filename}** | {role} — {base_capabilities}, **{version_features}** | {tools} |'. Template rules: (a) role = module's one-sentence role definition (stable across versions, derived from module summary first sentence), (b) base_capabilities = comma-separated core capabilities (grows with each evolution, old version-specific features demoted here), (c) version_features = bold-marked features added in current version (replaced each evolution), (d) tools = module's declared tools list. For Evolve mode: parse existing AIAP.md module table, identify role/base/version segments per row, demote previous version_features to base_capabilities (remove bold), insert new LEVEL_B features as bold version_features. For Create mode: generate initial template from module summary and description.
SaveDraft.step3: Output: { draft_path, test_report, traffic_report, pattern, version, weighted_score }.
SaveDraft.constraints: Unresolved RED -> prepend WARNING in draft metadata.
SaveDraft.Error: Write fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display JSON in code block -> inform.

ReflectionCheck.step1: Verify: all Mermaid nodes have functions? Any RED residuals? Evolution items implemented?
ReflectionCheck.step2: Generate reflection_notes for ReviewFlow. Residuals -> note for next cycle.
ReflectionCheck.Error: Verification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip advisory, set notes = empty -> inform.
